run_metadata.notes: Trust model: Dashboard `/api/*` is gated by `proxy.ts` (Bearer shared secret, optional Supabase JWT, or HMAC cookie). Repair FastAPI uses `REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET`. Supabase Edge Functions run outside that boundary: `ingest-audit` uses the service role with no request authentication in code—any caller who can reach the function URL can write. `github-webhook` relies on HMAC; `repair-callback` uses Bearer compare. Data plane: Postgres via Supabase; compromise of ingest or weak secrets affects audit/repair integrity.

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical auth, validation, secret, and data-exposure risks with realistic attack scenarios.

files_skipped: apps/dashboard/app/api/**/*.ts — sampled representative routes; auth is centralized in proxy.ts
services/repair/api/routes/*.py — protected by global Depends(require_auth) on /repair and /config
coverage.incomplete_reason: Stopped after documenting a P0 unauthenticated ingest surface per agent stop rule; remaining dashboard API routes not exhaustively reviewed.

findings.title: Ingest edge function has no caller authentication
findings.description: The `ingest-audit` Supabase Edge Function creates a `supabase-js` client with `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` and upserts `audit_runs`, `findings`, and `orchestration_events` from the request body. There is no verification of a shared secret, Supabase JWT, or other caller credential in the handler. Attack scenario: anyone who can invoke the deployed function URL (URL discovery, leaked project ref, or browser/CORS context if exposed) sends a crafted JSON body and overwrites or poisons audit results and findings for arbitrary `project_id` values, corrupting compliance data and downstream repair/orchestration decisions.

proof_hooks.summary: Handler uses service role and writes without auth check.

steps: Deploy or point at the ingest-audit function endpoint.
POST JSON with project_id and synthesizer_payload matching expected shape.
Observe 200 and rows inserted/updated in audit_runs/findings without any Authorization validation in code.

repro_steps: POST to the edge function URL with a valid synthesizer-shaped body.
Confirm writes succeed without presenting a secret or user JWT in application logic.
findings.impact: Full integrity loss for audit data tied to this pipeline: fake findings, deleted accountability, and wrong repair triggers for projects.

suggested_fix.approach: Require a server-only secret (compare timing-safe), or verify Supabase JWT with role checks, or disable public invocation and rely on Supabase function JWT verification in project config—ensure code and platform config agree.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Breaking change for any caller until they send the new credential.

history.notes: Evidence from static review of edge function source.

findings.title: Empty REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET accepts empty Bearer token
findings.description: `repair-callback` compares `Authorization` Bearer text to `Deno.env.get('REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET')`. If the secret is set to an empty string in deployment, `token !== expectedToken` is false when both are empty, so the handler accepts `Authorization: Bearer ` with no secret. Attack scenario: misconfiguration (empty env in Supabase secrets) leaves the callback open; an attacker invokes the function and updates arbitrary `repair_job_id` rows via the service-role client.

proof_hooks.summary: Bearer compared to env; no reject when secret missing or empty before compare.

repro_steps: Set REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET to empty in the function environment.
POST with Header Authorization: Bearer (empty token after prefix).
Observe 200 and repair_jobs update if id exists.
findings.impact: Unauthorized updates to repair job status, PR metadata, and orchestration_events linkage.

suggested_fix.approach: Reject early if `!expectedToken?.trim()` with 503; use timing-safe compare for secrets.

history.notes: Logic review of callback auth branch.

findings.title: penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API disables dashboard API authentication
findings.description: When neither `DASHBOARD_API_SECRET` nor `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` is set, production allows all `/api/*` traffic if `penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API` is `1` or `true`. `proxy.ts` logs a warning but still calls `next()`. Attack scenario: operator mistake or compromised env leaves the dashboard API fully open; any client can read or mutate project state exposed by those routes with no shared secret or user session.

proof_hooks.summary: Open API branch returns next() for all API routes.
Flag enables open API in production when no secrets.

repro_steps: Unset DASHBOARD_API_SECRET and SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET in a production-like build.
Call any /api/* route without Authorization and observe success.
findings.impact: Unauthorized access to dashboard-backed data and actions (project CRUD, orchestration, imports) depending on route behavior.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove or gate `penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API` behind explicit local-only hostname; fail closed in production regardless of flag; alert on startup.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: May break intentional staging setups; document migration.

history.notes: Documented intentional DX escape hatch with production hazard.

findings.title: Bearer secrets compared with plain equality
findings.description: Repair service `require_auth` and the repair-callback use `!=` / `!==` for bearer tokens. Attack scenario: local timing side channels are usually low risk on cloud runtimes, but network attackers with very fine-grained timing to the same instance could theoretically learn bytes; primary practical concern is consistency with crypto best practices for long-lived shared secrets.
findings.impact: Marginal risk of secret byte leakage via timing; defense-in-depth gap versus constant-time compare.

suggested_fix.approach: Use timing-safe comparison (e.g. `hmac.compare_digest` in Python; SubtleCrypto or hex compare loop in Deno) after normalizing encoding once.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: None significant if only fixing comparison.

history.notes: Pairing Python and Deno bearer checks.

next_actions.action: Add authentication or platform-level JWT verification to ingest-audit before any service-role write.
next_actions.rationale: P0 integrity: unauthenticated service-role ingest is the widest blast radius.

next_actions.action: Fail closed when REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET is missing or empty in repair-callback.
Restrict or remove penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API for production deployments.
next_actions.rationale: Removes accidental full API exposure from env mistakes.

next_actions.action: Replace string equality with timing-safe secret compare on repair paths.
next_actions.rationale: Low-cost hardening after critical auth paths are fixed.
